Name: kernel_optimization_for_large_models
Title: Kernel Optimization for Efficient Inference in Large-Scale Machine Learning Models
Short Hypothesis: By systematically profiling and optimizing kernel execution for large-scale models deployed in real-world settings, we can achieve significant improvements in latency and throughput that are not currently realized in existing literature.
Related Work: Existing works primarily focus on CUDA optimizations and specific kernel improvements (e.g., reduction kernels, depthwise convolutions). However, they often lack a comprehensive framework that systematically profiles kernel execution in real-world conditions with a focus on large models. This proposal distinguishes itself by emphasizing on-the-ground profiling and end-to-end optimizations rather than isolated kernel enhancements.
Abstract: As machine learning models grow in size and complexity, ensuring efficient inference becomes paramount for practical deployment. This proposal aims to develop a comprehensive framework that systematically profiles kernel execution of large-scale models on GPUs, focusing on real-world deployment scenarios. By leveraging advanced profiling tools (e.g., nvprof, Nsight) and targeted optimizations (e.g., memory coalescing, kernel fusion), we aim to uncover bottlenecks that hinder performance. Our approach emphasizes empirical measurements of latency and throughput, providing a means to enhance inference efficiency across various model architectures. We propose experiments that will validate our framework's effectiveness, with the expectation of achieving substantial speedups compared to existing state-of-the-art solutions. This research not only aims to contribute to the field of machine learning systems engineering but also provides practical insights for practitioners aiming to deploy large models efficiently.

Experiments.description: Profile existing large-scale model implementations using standard kernels to establish baseline performance metrics.
Implement targeted optimizations based on profiling results, such as memory coalescing and shared memory usage enhancements.
Conduct end-to-end benchmarking of optimized models against baseline models in fixed accuracy constraints.

Risk Factors and Limitations: Risks include potential overfitting of optimizations to specific hardware configurations, leading to less generalizable results. Limited access to diverse hardware setups may restrict the applicability of findings. Additionally, measuring energy efficiency accurately can be complex, potentially confounding results.
